Title: Activate shipping method
Steps:
1. Navigate to Store > Shipping Methods
2. Select a shipping method
3. Activate it
4. Save
Expected Result: Shipping method activated and available for checkout

Title: Deactivate shipping method
Steps:
1. Select active shipping method
2. Deactivate
3. Save
Expected Result: Shipping method deactivated; not available for checkout

Title: Configure ground and air shipping rates
Steps:
1. Open shipping method settings
2. Set Ground shipping rate
3. Set Air shipping rate
4. Save
Expected Result: Shipping rates saved; applied correctly to orders

Title: Save and reset shipping rate changes
Steps:
1. Modify shipping rate
2. Click Save
3. Modify again
4. Click Reset
Expected Result: Save persists changes; Reset reverts to last saved state

Title: Shipping tax type configuration
Steps:
1. Set Tax type to FR020100
2. Verify taxes calculated without shipping value
3. Remove tax type
4. Verify taxes calculated with shipping value
Expected Result: Tax calculations respect shipping tax type configuration

Title: Enable BOPIS shipping method for store
Steps:
1. Navigate to Store > Shipping
2. Enable BuyOnlinePickupInStore method
3. Save
Expected Result: BOPIS shipping method enabled for the store

Title: Add new pickup location
Steps:
1. Navigate to BOPIS settings
2. Click Add Pickup Location
3. Fill in name and address details
4. Transfer fulfillment centers
5. Save
Expected Result: New pickup location created with FFC assignment

Title: Edit pickup location
Steps:
1. Open existing pickup location
2. Edit fields
3. Save
Expected Result: Pickup location updated successfully

Title: Delete pickup location
Steps:
1. Select pickup location
2. Click Delete
3. Confirm
Expected Result: Pickup location deleted

Title: Validate pickup location empty name
Steps:
1. Add new pickup location
2. Leave Name field empty
3. Attempt to save
Expected Result: Validation error shown; save prevented

Title: Multiple selection in transfer fulfillment centers
Steps:
1. Open pickup location
2. Select multiple fulfillment centers
3. Transfer
Expected Result: Multiple FFCs transferred to pickup location

Title: Delete multiple fulfillment centers from pickup location
Steps:
1. Select multiple transferred FFCs
2. Delete selection
Expected Result: Selected FFCs removed from pickup location

Title: Enable/disable pickup location active status
Steps:
1. Toggle Active status OFF
2. Save
3. Toggle Active status ON
4. Save
Expected Result: Active status toggles correctly; affects frontend availability

Title: Search pickup locations
Steps:
1. Search by valid input
2. Search by partial input
3. Search with special characters
4. Search non-existing location
5. Verify case insensitivity
Expected Result: Search works with all input types; case insensitive

Title: Verify pickup locations on frontend
Steps:
1. Navigate to storefront checkout
2. Select BOPIS shipping
3. Verify pickup locations displayed
Expected Result: Active pickup locations appear on storefront for selection

Title: Shipping Method Priority Ordering
Preconditions: Multiple shipping methods active; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Store > Shipping Methods
2. Identify at least 3 active shipping methods
3. Set Priority value to 10 on the first shipping method and save
4. Set Priority value to 20 on the second shipping method and save
5. Set Priority value to 30 on the third shipping method and save
6. Return to the Shipping Methods list view
7. Refresh the page
8. Observe the display order of shipping methods in the list
Expected Result:
- Priority field accepts numeric values for each shipping method
- Each priority change is saved successfully
- Shipping methods list displays methods in ascending priority order (lower number = higher priority)
- Method with priority 10 appears before method with priority 20
- Method with priority 20 appears before method with priority 30
- Display order reflects the configured priority on page refresh

Title: Multi-Warehouse Shipping Calculation
Preconditions: Multiple warehouses configured; products assigned to different warehouses; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Orders > Create New Order or use a test order
2. Add Product A which is stocked in Warehouse 1 to the cart
3. Add Product B which is stocked in Warehouse 2 to the cart
4. Proceed to the shipping step
5. Observe the shipping options and rate calculation
6. Select a shipping method and proceed to checkout
7. Review the order summary for shipping line items
8. Confirm the order and review the created order record in Admin
Expected Result:
- Both products from different warehouses are added to the same order without errors
- Shipping calculation accounts for fulfillment from 2 separate warehouses
- Shipping rate reflects split-shipment logic if configured (separate rates per warehouse, or combined)
- Order is confirmed successfully
- Order record shows correct shipping assignment and cost
- No incorrect zero-rate or null shipping calculation

Title: Free Shipping Threshold Configuration
Preconditions: Shipping method active; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Store > Shipping Methods
2. Open the target shipping method settings
3. Locate the Free Shipping Threshold or Minimum Order Amount field
4. Enter a threshold value of 100 (USD or store currency)
5. Click Save
6. Navigate to the storefront and add items with total value below $100 to cart
7. Proceed to checkout and observe the shipping rate for the configured method
8. Add more items to bring the cart total above $100 and re-check the shipping rate
Expected Result:
- Threshold field accepts numeric value 100
- Settings saved without errors
- Cart total below $100 shows the normal shipping rate for the method
- Cart total at or above $100 shows $0.00 (free shipping) for the method
- Free shipping label or indicator is displayed to the customer when threshold is met
- Other shipping methods are not affected by this threshold setting

Title: Shipping Rate by Weight Configuration
Preconditions: Shipping method active; products with weight attributes configured; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Store > Shipping Methods
2. Open the target shipping method settings
3. Locate the Rate Configuration section and select Weight-Based pricing
4. Add a weight tier: 0–1 kg = $5.00 rate
5. Add a second weight tier: 1–5 kg = $10.00 rate
6. Add a third weight tier: 5+ kg = $20.00 rate
7. Save the shipping method configuration
8. Create a test order with a product weighing 0.5 kg and verify the shipping rate
9. Repeat with a product weighing 3 kg and verify the rate
Expected Result:
- Weight-based pricing tiers are saved correctly for each tier
- Order with 0.5 kg product calculates shipping at $5.00
- Order with 3 kg product calculates shipping at $10.00
- Tier boundaries are applied correctly (inclusive/exclusive boundary as per system design)
- Weight calculation uses the product's configured weight attribute
- Rates are reflected correctly in the order summary

Title: Shipping Zone Configuration
Preconditions: Shipping module installed; countries/regions available; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Store > Shipping > Zones (or equivalent navigation)
2. Click Add Zone or Create New Zone
3. Enter zone name 'Europe Zone'
4. Select at least 3 countries to include in the zone (e.g. Germany, France, Netherlands)
5. Save the zone configuration
6. Navigate back to Shipping Methods
7. Open an existing active shipping method
8. Assign the newly created 'Europe Zone' to the shipping method
9. Save the shipping method
10. Verify the zone appears in the shipping method's zone assignments
Expected Result:
- Zone creation form accepts name 'Europe Zone'
- Country selection works for all selected countries
- Zone is saved and appears in the zones list
- Shipping method assignment to the zone is saved successfully
- Zone assignment is visible on the shipping method detail page
- Shipping method is only available for orders with a delivery address in one of the assigned zone countries
- Shipping method does not appear for addresses outside the configured zone
